summary: The case shows five modifiable levers driving distress: intrusive visual replay (C01), persistent rumination (C02), acute physiological reactivity to evaluative cues (C03), anticipatory fear of humiliation (C04), and a lowered threshold for perceiving criticism (C05). The proposed BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers span cognitive-behavioral techniques, autonomic regulation, social exposure, cognitive bias modification, sleep and mindfulness, and social support to reduce re-experiencing, worry, arousal, and threat appraisals. Interventions are designed as changeable predictors (levers) with concrete measurement, feasible delivery, and a clear path to cross-domain gains (e.g., improved cognitive control via sleep + mindfulness can dampen rumination and reactivity; social exposure can reduce anticipatory fear while improving tolerance to feedback). A multi-pronged, 6–8 week rollout with consistent measurement is recommended, prioritizing high-impact, high-feasibility interventions first.

solutions.solution_candidate: Imagery-based CBT with imagery rescripting to re-contextualize the boss-yelling memory and reduce involuntary re-experiencing. Includes a brief, guided daily practice (15–20 minutes) and 2–3 weekly therapist-assisted or self-guided sessions focusing on reframing the scene, decreasing vividness, and creating alternative non-humiliating appraisals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Modifies the memory representation via reconsolidation-like processes and inhibitory learning, reducing vividness and emotional intensity of the intrusive clip.
solutions.measurement: Daily intrusion diary (frequency 0–4+), imagery vividness rating (0–100), distress during recall (0–10); weekly composite score.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured rumination management using scheduled cognitive-behavioral strategies: fixed worry periods (e.g., 3x/day for 10 minutes), cognitive defusion, and problem-solving training to redirect attention away from unproductive dwelling.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces perseverative cognition by design, improving cognitive flexibility and reducing time spent in repetitive verbal rehearsal.
solutions.measurement: Ruminative Responses Scale (RRS) scores; daily diary of time spent ruminating (minutes); distress during rumination (0–10).

solutions.solution_candidate: Autonomic regulation via paced breathing with optional HRV biofeedback and brief mindfulness cues to normalize physiological responses to evaluative feedback.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances autonomic balance (vagal tone) reducing stomach drop and arousal in response to feedback cues.
solutions.measurement: Self-reported arousal during feedback (0–10); HRV metrics (RMSSD) during tasks; resting HRV baseline.

solutions.solution_candidate: Graded social exposure with cognitive reappraisal: start with low-stakes feedback encounters, gradually increase complexity while practicing reframe strategies to reduce anticipatory fear of humiliation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Habituation to evaluative situations and corrective learning reduces anticipatory anxiety and perceived threat from social feedback.
solutions.measurement: anticipatory anxiety ratings (0–10) before exposures; social functioning questionnaire; exposure task success rate.
solutions.safety_notes: Normal safety planning for exposure tasks in case of distress; therapist supervision recommended.

solutions.solution_candidate: Interpretation bias modification and threat-interpretation retraining using computerized tasks and therapist-guided reflections to reduce hypervigilance to criticism.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Recalibrates threat appraisal to neutral/benign cues, reducing perceived aggressiveness of subtle feedback.
solutions.measurement: Interpretation bias task scores; self-reported threat perception during mild feedback (0–10); reaction time biases.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep optimization program to improve overall cognitive control and emotional regulation: consistent bed/wake times, morning light exposure, caffeine reduction after noon, and a brief wind-down routine.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep supports executive function and emotional regulation, attenuating rumination and physiological reactivity.
solutions.measurement: Sleep diaries (bed/wake times, duration); PSQI score; subjective alertness (0–10) daily.

solutions.solution_candidate: Mindfulness-based strategies (8-week program or 4-week abbreviated MBCT) to cultivate nonjudgmental awareness of thoughts and bodily sensations, reducing reactivity, rumination, and intrusive imagery.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances metacognitive awareness and autonomic regulation, decreasing automatic reactivity to intrusive imagery and feedback.
solutions.measurement: MAAS/FFMQ scores; daily distress during exposure to feedback; intrusion vividness (0–100).

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured social support and feedback safety-net: pairing with a trusted colleague or coach for weekly check-ins, feedback debriefs, and peer modeling to reduce humiliation fear and sensitivity to criticism.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support reduces social threat salience, provides corrective feedback in a safe space, and improves interpretation of feedback cues.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale; frequency of supportive interactions; self-rated threat from feedback (0–10).
